args.query:
SELECT "Index",
       SUM(CASE WHEN "Close" > "Open" THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) AS up_days,
       SUM(CASE WHEN "Close" < "Open" THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) AS down_days,
       SUM(CASE WHEN "Close" = "Open" THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) AS flat_days,
       COUNT(*) AS total_days
FROM index_trade
WHERE "Index" IN ('NYA','GSPTSE','IXIC') AND "Date" LIKE '2018-%'
GROUP BY "Index";

exec_args.sql:
SELECT "Index",
       SUM(CASE WHEN "Close" > "Open" THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) AS up_days,
       SUM(CASE WHEN "Close" < "Open" THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) AS down_days,
       SUM(CASE WHEN "Close" = "Open" THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) AS flat_days,
       COUNT(*) AS total_days
FROM index_trade
WHERE "Index" IN ('NYA','GSPTSE','IXIC') AND "Date" LIKE '2018-%'
GROUP BY "Index";

result.preview:
"The North American indices with more up days than down days in 2018 were:

- IXIC (NASDAQ Composite): 44 up days, 31 down days
- GSPTSE (S&P/TSX Composite): 44 up days, 41 down days

(NYA had 37 up days and 42 down days.)"
